rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Dale Duncan And Marta Munoz Mendoza, 3rd Party Judgment Creditors Of Defendants, For Order Directing Receiver To List And Market Receivership Property For Sale (Hill Street)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 09, 2022 line 1. THIRD PARTY DALE DUNCAN, MARTA MENDOZA Notice Of Motion And Motion By Dale Duncan And Marta Munoz Mendoza, 3rd Party Judgment Creditors Of Defendants, For Order Directing Receiver To List And Market Receivership Property For Sale (Hill Street)is continued to May 16, 2022 to be heard with other pending motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: OASIS GRILL, LLC ET AL VS. ERIC KETCHUM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Ketchum'S Motion To Compel Further Answers To Propounded Discovery And For Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 09, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANT ERIC KETCHUM Notice Of Ketchum'S Motion To Compel Further Answers To Propounded Discovery And For Sanction is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to Interrogatories 153, 154, 163, and 164; Requests for Admissions 138-145, 148, 150-152. Motion is otherwise DENIED. Requests for sanctions are DENIED. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: FELL HOLDINGS LLC VS. FELL ST AUTOMOTIVE CLINIC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 09, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANT LAURENCE NASEY HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION Hearing Required. Any evidence claiming intends to present at the hearing must be served on the Plaintiff no later than 8:00 a.m. on May 9, 2022 (personally or electronically). Courtesy copies of any evidence to be presented to the court (e.g. rental agreement with the plaintiff; rental agreement with the former owner, proof of occupancy by Mr. Nasey, an individual etc) must be delivered no later than 9:00 a.m, on May 9, 2022 and handed to the Courtroom clerks. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: KRISS MIRANDA VS. PEARLY R.L. MARTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Motion To Quash; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 09, 2022 line 9. DEFENDANT PEARLY MARTIN Defendants Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Motion To Quash; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities is GRANTED. Motion is properly set per CCP 1167.4 and served by mail. Plaintiff may not obtain summons based on the filing currently before the Court. Plaintiff may amend UD-100/UD-101 to reconcile allegations in paragraph 3b of form UD-101 and paragraph 6a of form UD-100 if Plaintiff can do so in good faith. If the rental agreement at issue in this action was entered into on the date indicated in UD-100, then paragraph 3(b) of UD-100 shall be filled out by checking "Yes." Plaintiff shall have 3 days from the notice of entry of order to make any amendment. Plaintiff shall present amended pleadings to the Court (may be done via ex-parte) seeking an order directing the clerk to issue amended summons. The Clerk shall not issue summons in this matter without further order of this Court. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
